Google and Apple “generously” allowing apps to use alternative payment systems but only dropping their cut from 30% to 27% (leaving apps with no gain since Stripe charges 2.9%) is the most cynical move in big tech since Apple pulled the ATT privacy scam. https://techcrunch.com/...

Congratulations to Google, fast-following Apple to introduce fake open billing in Europe - “allowing” competing payment services but eliminating their ability to compete by demanding 27% of revenue. https://twitter.com/...

This is why today, as part of our compliance efforts, we have announced a new billing programme which will allow developers to offer alternatives to Google Play's billing system to users in the EEA. You can read more here. https://blog.google/...

you could slap a Disney+ or Netflix or HBO Max logo on this and nobody would be able to tell it's a different app https://twitter.com/...

What they're really saying is that with plateauing growth and increased competition they need to find creative new ways to nickel and dime customers into paying more money for the same service if they're to keep the insatiable wall street maw well fed

not only did Netflix spend a decade clearly stating password sharing was no big deal, they're already limiting the number of concurrent, active streams per account, AND they just imposed a major new price hike.

As @KarlBode notes, Netflix users *already* pay for 1/2/4 concurrent streams. So now you aren't just paying for how many concurrent streams you make, but where they're coming from. Here comes the new cable, same as the old cable. https://twitter.com/...

Netflix is now charging users for additional usage outside of your home for more than 2 weeks in five South American countries ($1.17 - $2.99). There's an exception for vacations, once per location per year. Ads and now this. Growth's definitely done. https://www.theverge.com/...
That's gonna definitely upset the college crowd. Anyone have Netflix subscription on their back-to-school shopping list? https://twitter.com/...
I see Netflix is doing their best to promote piracy yet again. https://twitter.com/...
Netflix testing ways to drive VPN users and travelers back to BitTorrent. https://twitter.com/...
Other Services: make fewer, better shows with less money Netflix: ok let's just *really* piss our users off https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Netflix is daring people to cancel with moves like this. My guess is a lot of them will. https://twitter.com/...

“But today's widespread account sharing between households undermines our long term ability to invest in and improve our service.” Netflix sounds more like Comcast every day. https://www.theverge.com/...

BitSight: a popular GPS vehicle tracker made by Shenzhen-based Micodus can be easily exploited to track and remotely cut the engines of at least 1M vehicles
Micodus GPS Flaws Would Let Hackers Monitor Vehicle Movements
GPS trackers used for vehicle fleet management can be hijacked by hackers
Attackers can surveil, disrupt vehicles outfitted with popular GPS tracker, CISA warns

BitSight said the six flaws include hardcoded and default passwords ("123456"), which can be used to hijack any GPS tracker and track vehicles in real-time. Governments, militaries, Fortune 50 giants, and a nuclear power plant operator are all affected. https://techcrunch.com/...

New: Security flaws in a widely used Micodus GPS vehicle tracker is exposing over 1 million vehicle locations around the world, and can be easily exploited to remotely cut engines. Worse: there's currently no fix. https://techcrunch.com/...

The US should grant more green cards to retain tech leaders; Turkish MIT and Caltech grad Erdal Arikan, whose work led to Huawei's 5G lead, had to leave the US

We blew our ability to lead in the development of and setting standards for the 5G technology... by not granting **ONE** Green Card. Imagine all the technology we're saying goodbye to by not retaining the foreign STEM talent trained at US universities. https://foreignpolicy.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Immigration is the United States' secret sauce—including in its competition with China. https://foreignpolicy.com/...

Why do foreign students applying to US universities have to convince @StateDept they'll return home after graduating? Surely US should staple a green card to the certificate of any post-grad from a top US university? You *want* them to return to China? https://foreignpolicy.com/...

How the U.S. forgot immigration was a key advantage blows my mind... but then I remember 30% of the adult U.S. population believes the Sun revolves the Earth and I remember why (mind still blown of course). https://twitter.com/...
It's beyond comprehension how the United States ended up with a system where illegal immigration is welcomed, while legal immigration is extremely difficult and cumbersome. https://twitter.com/...

Former Google CEO (and big Biden donor) Eric Schmidt with some advice in Foreign Policy: “Joe Biden should launch an urgent drive to recruit and retain 1 million tech superstars from around the world by the end of his first term in office.” https://foreignpolicy.com/...
I'd say not just the US but all the liberal democracies in the world. https://twitter.com/...

President Biden should announce a commitment to recruit 1 million of the world's most technically talented individuals to the US by January 2025 - ⁦@ericschmidt⁩ and ⁦Graham Allison https://foreignpolicy.com/...
This was a platform of the Republican party for half my life. No idea how “make America technologically competitive” became untouchable by both parties. https://twitter.com/...

US losing out on talents every minute to not only adversaries but allies. Who's listening? Besides deep anti-immigration bias, politicians can even drag down law designed to help US chiptech in favor of partisan politics. Soon US won't be top destination for tech talents anymore! https://twitter.com/...

Your periodic reminder that neither party has done shit to fix immigration in ages. Just a fundraising/attention grabbing talking point. And putting aside the human cost, and it is not small, it costs the country actual money as well. https://twitter.com/...
On a risk reward basis the US would just be better off giving green cards to people who graduate from CalTech and MIT. https://twitter.com/...

You wonder why China is the leader in 5G? Immigration policy ! Read what we need to do to keep America winning and not losing; we desperately need to get the top technical talent from everywhere else into the US NOW https://foreignpolicy.com/...

To leverage the United States' greatest advantage over China, the Biden administration should recruit 1 million foreign scientists, programmers, and engineers, Graham Allison and Eric Schmidt write. https://foreignpolicy.com/...
Several historic sites in Nepal, where TikTok creators have reportedly trampled crops and even caused traffic jams while filming, have erected “No TikTok” signs

From Lumbini to Boudhanath Stupa, popular religious sites in Nepal have banned TikTok creators for creating a nuisance. Freedom of expression activists believe the prohibitions are unfair https://restofworld.org/...

Prominent tourist and religious sites in Nepal are putting up “No TikTok” signs to keep creators from shooting at the premises. My story for @restofworld. Thanks to @ItikaSPunit @AnupKaphle @sabrinatoppa @mvzelenks https://restofworld.org/...
